issue.title: Consider blocksdir when estimating available disk space to determine default pruning
issue.body:
When running bitcoin-qt for the first time, the intro window is shown where the user is prompted for where they want to store the datadir, the default is `~/.bitcoin`.

If there's not a lot of space in `~/.bitcoin`, the prune option is enabled by default. However, a common use case is to point `-blocksdir` to a larger partition/drive.

The current behavior as of Bitcoin Core v31.1 is to [check for the available space of the datadir](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/gui/blob/master/src/qt/freespacechecker.cpp) and prune gets enabled by default even when `-blocksdir` is being pointed to a larger drive.

It would be nice to have bitcoin-qt consider blocksdir being on a separate path/partition/drive before enabling the prune option. Additionally, this sounds similar to #709 although I'm not entirely sure it's the same issue.
